ADC_LABEL_SETUP: Setup (this is required to use the ADC read block)
ADC_CONFIGURE_MESSAGE0: configure ADC on pin $[PIN] with attenuation $[ATTEN]
ADC_CONFIGURE_TOOLTIP: Configure the ADC on the specified pin with the given attenuation
ADC_READ_TOOLTIP: Read the ADC value from the specified pin
CONTROL_LABEL_EXIT: Exit the program with a specific exit code
CONTROL_LABEL_DATE: Get the current time in milliseconds since the program started
CONTROL_EXIT_TOOLTIP: Exit the program with the specified exit code
CONTROL_DATENOW_TOOLTIP: Get the current time in milliseconds since the program started
GPIO_LABEL_SETUP: Setup (this is required to use other GPIO blocks)
GPIO_PINMODE_TOOLTIP: Set the mode of the specified pin
GPIO_WRITE_TOOLTIP: Write a digital value to the specified pin
GPIO_READ_TOOLTIP: Read the digital value from the specified pin
GPIO_ONEVENT_TOOLTIP: Set an event handler for the specified pin and event type
GPIO_OFFEVENT_TOOLTIP: Remove the event handler for the specified pin and event type
I2C_LABEL_SETUP: Setup (this is required to use other I2C blocks)
I2C_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0:
create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] on $[I2C] with
 SDA pin $[SDA] SCL pin $[SCL]
 bitrate $[BITRATE]
I2C_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Setup the I2C bus with specified SDA and SCL pins and bitrate
I2C_LABEL_IN_FUTURE_RELEASES: I2C Read/Write will be added in future releases.
KEYVALUE_DESCRIPTION: Key value storage blocks for storing key-value pairs in non-volatile storage.
KEYVALUE_LABEL_COMMIT: Committing changes to storage (without this, any changes will not be saved)
KV_OPEN_TOOLTIP: Initialize an Key Value Storage namespace
KV_SET_STRING_MESSAGE0: set KV $[KV_INSTANCE] key $[KEY] to string value $[VALUE]
KV_SET_STRING_TOOLTIP: Set a string value in NVS for the specified key
KV_SET_NUMBER_MESSAGE0: set KV $[KV_INSTANCE] key $[KEY] to number value $[VALUE]
KV_SET_NUMBER_TOOLTIP: Set a number value in NVS for the specified key
KV_GET_STRING_TOOLTIP: Get a string value from NVS for the specified key
KV_GET_NUMBER_TOOLTIP: Get a number value from NVS for the specified key
KV_ERASE_TOOLTIP: Erase the specified key from NVS
KV_EXISTS_TOOLTIP: Check if the specified key exists in NVS
KV_COMMIT_TOOLTIP: Commit any pending changes to NVS
MOTOR_LABEL_SETUP: Creating a Motor instance (example configuration from Robutek V2)
MOTOR_LABEL_REQUIRED_CONFIG: Speed and ramp configuration is by default set to 0. Configure it...
MOTOR_CONSTRUCTOR_PINS_MESSAGE0: motor pins motA $[MOT_A] motB $[MOT_B] encA $[ENC_A] encB $[ENC_B]
MOTOR_CONSTRUCTOR_REGPARAMS_MESSAGE0:
reg params kp $[KP] ki $[KI] kd $[KD]
 kv $[KV] ka $[KA] kc $[KC]
 maxIOut $[MAX_IOUT]
 unwindFactor $[UNWIND_FACTOR]
MOTOR_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0:
create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] motor with 
pins $[PINS]
reg params $[REG_PARAMS]
 encoder ticks $[ENC_TICKS] circumference $[CIRCUMFERENCE] (mm)
MOTOR_SETSPEED_TOOLTIP: Set the speed of the motor
MOTOR_SETRAMP_TOOLTIP: Set the speed ramp of the motor
MOTOR_MOVE_DISTANCE_TOOLTIP: Move the motor for the specified distance
MOTOR_MOVE_TIME_TOOLTIP: Move the motor for the specified time
MOTOR_MOVE_INFINITE_TOOLTIP: Move the motor indefinitely until another command is given
MOTOR_SETRAW_MESSAGE0: set raw power of $[MOTOR_INSTANCE] to $[POWER] (-1023 to 1023)
MOTOR_SETRAW_TOOLTIP: Set raw power to the motor
MOTOR_GETPOSITION_TOOLTIP: Get the position of the motor
PULSECOUNTER_LABEL_SETUP: Setup (this is required to use other Pulse Counter blocks)
PULSECOUNTER_LABEL_SETUP_DETAILS:
The edge pin detects signal pulses (rising/falling edges). The level pin modifies counting behavior based on its logic level.
Edge actions (Hold/Increase/Decrease) define what happens on each edge.
Level actions (Keep/Inverse/Hold) override edge actions depending on the level pin state.
The counter starts automatically after creation. Note: Not available on ESP32-C3.
PC_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0:
create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] pulse counter
 on level pin $[PIN_LEVEL] edge pin $[PIN_EDGE]
 with level low action $[LEVEL_LOW_ACTION]
 level high action $[LEVEL_HIGH_ACTION]
 edge positive action $[EDGE_POS_ACTION]
 edge negative action $[EDGE_NEG_ACTION]
PC_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a new Pulse Counter instance
PC_READ_TOOLTIP: Read the current value of the pulse counter
SR_BEGIN_TOOLTIP: Initialize the SimpleRadio on the specified group
SR_SENDKEYVALUE_TOOLTIP: Send a key-value pair via SimpleRadio
SR_ONRECEIVENUMBER_TOOLTIP: Register a callback for receiving numbers via SimpleRadio. Use the receivedNumber block inside to access the value.
SR_ONRECEIVESTRING_TOOLTIP: Register a callback for receiving strings via SimpleRadio. Use the receivedString block inside to access the value.
SR_ONRECEIVEKEYVALUE_TOOLTIP: Register a callback for receiving key-value pairs via SimpleRadio. Use the receivedKey, receivedValue blocks inside to access the values.
SR_OFFRECEIVE_TOOLTIP: Unregister the callback for the specified SimpleRadio packet type
SR_SENDJSON_TOOLTIP: Convert an object to JSON text and send it over SimpleRadio.
SR_ONRECEIVEJSON_TOOLTIP: Listen for JSON text packets, parse them, and run the attached code.
STREAM_STDOUT_WRITE_TOOLTIP: Write text directly to stdout without adding a new line.
STREAM_STDERR_WRITE_TOOLTIP: Write text directly to stderr without adding a new line.
STREAM_ONCHAR_MESSAGE0:
when a character is read from standard input do
$[CODE]
STREAM_ONCHAR_TOOLTIP: Continuously read one character at a time from standard input and run the attached code.
STREAM_ONCHUNK_MESSAGE0:
when a chunk is read from standard input do
$[CODE]
STREAM_ONCHUNK_TOOLTIP: Continuously read chunks from standard input and run the attached code.
SPI_LABEL_SETUP: Setup (this is required to use other SPI blocks)
SPI_SETUP_MESSAGE0:
create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] on $[SPI] with
 SCK pin $[SCK] MOSI pin $[MOSI] MISO pin $[MISO]
 baud $[BAUD] mode $[MODE] order $[ORDER]
SPI_SETUP_TOOLTIP: Setup an SPI instance with selected bus and pin/options
SPI_SEND_TOOLTIP: Send data over SPI and return received bytes
SPI_TRANSFER_MESSAGE0:
$[SPI_INSTANCE] transfer $[DATA] with CS pin $[CS]
 read length $[RX_LENGTH] with QIO $[QIO]
SPI_TRANSFER_TOOLTIP: Perform SPI write+read transfer and return read bytes
UDP_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0:
create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] UDP socket
 on address $[ADDRESS] port $[PORT]
UDP_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create and bind a UDP socket to the given address and port
UDP_READ_TOOLTIP: Read one datagram from UDP socket (returns null when none is available)
UDP_WRITE_TOOLTIP: Send one UDP datagram to destination address and port
SMARTLED_LABEL_SETUP: Setup (this is required to use other SmartLED blocks)
SMARTLED_LABEL_SHOW: Show LEDs (update strip to show set colors)
STRIP_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] strip on pin $[LED_PIN] with $[LED_COUNT] LEDs of type $[LED_TYPE]
STRIP_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a new Smart LED strip instance
STRIP_SET_TOOLTIP: Set the LED at the specified index to the given color
STRIP_SHOW_TOOLTIP: Update the LED strip to show the set colors
STRIP_GET_TOOLTIP: Get the color of the LED at the specified index
STRIP_CLEAR_TOOLTIP: Clear the LED strip (set all LEDs to off)
WIFI_CURRENTIP_TOOLTIP: Get the current IP address of the WiFi connection
WIFI_WAITFORCONNECTION_TOOLTIP: Wait until the device is connected to a WiFi network
COMMUNICATION_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for various communication protocols and interfaces.
PROTOCOLS_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for communication protocols like SPI, I2C, etc.
PERIPHERALS_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for various hardware peripherals like sensors, motors, etc.
MOVEMENT_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for controlling movement, such as motors and servos.
PWM_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] PWM on pin $[PIN] with frequency $[FREQUENCY] Hz resolution $[RESOLUTION] bits and duty $[DUTY]
PWM_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a new PWM output instance
VARIABLE_PWM_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] variable PWM on pin $[PIN] with frequency $[FREQUENCY] Hz resolution $[RESOLUTION] bits and duty $[DUTY]
VARIABLE_PWM_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a new variable PWM output instance with adjustable frequency and resolution
PWM_SETDUTY_TOOLTIP: Set the duty cycle of the PWM output
VARIABLE_PWM_SETDUTY_TOOLTIP: Set the duty cycle of the variable PWM output
VARIABLE_PWM_SETFREQUENCY_TOOLTIP: Set the frequency of the variable PWM output
VARIABLE_PWM_SETRESOLUTION_TOOLTIP: Set the resolution of the variable PWM output
RENDERER_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for scenes, shapes, textures, fonts, and rendering into a framebuffer.
RENDERER_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a renderer for a framebuffer with the given size.
RENDERER_COLLECTION_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] scene x $[X] y $[Y] color $[COLOR] z $[Z]
RENDERER_COLLECTION_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a collection that can hold shapes and be rendered as a scene.
RENDERER_CIRCLE_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] circle x $[X] y $[Y] radius $[RADIUS] color $[COLOR] fill $[FILL]
RENDERER_RECTANGLE_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] rectangle x $[X] y $[Y] width $[WIDTH] height $[HEIGHT] color $[COLOR] fill $[FILL]
RENDERER_POINT_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] point x $[X] y $[Y] color $[COLOR] z $[Z]
RENDERER_LINE_SEGMENT_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAG: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] line from x $[X] y $[Y] to x $[X2] y $[Y2] color $[COLOR] z $[Z]
RENDERER_REGULAR_POLYGON_CONSTRUCTOR_MES: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] regular polygon x $[X] y $[Y] sides $[SIDES] radius $[RADIUS] color $[COLOR] fill $[FILL]
RENDERER_REGULAR_POLYGON_CONSTRUCTOR_TOO: Create a regular polygon shape using a radius.
RENDERER_REGULAR_POLYGON_SIDE_CONSTRUCTO: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] regular polygon x $[X] y $[Y] sides $[SIDES] side length $[SIDE_LENGTH] color $[COLOR] fill $[FILL]
Create a regular polygon shape using a side length.
RENDERER_POLYGON_CONSTRUCTOR_MESSAGE0: create $[CONSTRUCTED_VAR_NAME] polygon x $[X] y $[Y] vertices $[VERTICES] color $[COLOR] fill $[FILL]
RENDERER_POLYGON_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a polygon shape from a list of [x, y] vertices.
RENDERER_COLLECTION_ADD_TOOLTIP: Add a shape to a scene collection.
RENDERER_COLLECTION_CLEAR_TOOLTIP: Remove all shapes from a collection.
RENDERER_RENDER_MESSAGE0: render $[COLLECTION_INSTANCE] with $[RENDERER_INSTANCE] into $[BUFFER] antialias $[ANTIALIAS] format $[FORMAT] rotation $[ROTATION]
RENDERER_RENDER_TOOLTIP: Render a scene collection into an ArrayBuffer.
RENDERER_DRAW_TEXT_MESSAGE0: draw text with $[RENDERER_INSTANCE] into $[BUFFER] text $[TEXT] x $[X] y $[Y] font $[FONT_INSTANCE] color $[COLOR] wrap $[WRAP] format $[FORMAT] rotation $[ROTATION]
RENDERER_DRAW_TEXT_TOOLTIP: Draw text directly into an ArrayBuffer.
RENDERER_SHAPE_TRANSLATE_TOOLTIP: Move a shape relative to its current position.
RENDERER_SHAPE_ROTATE_TOOLTIP: Rotate a shape around its pivot.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_PIVOT_TOOLTIP: Set the pivot point used for rotations.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_SCALE_MESSAGE0: scale $[SHAPE_INSTANCE] x $[SCALE_X] y $[SCALE_Y] origin x $[ORIGIN_X] y $[ORIGIN_Y]
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_SCALE_TOOLTIP: Set shape scale and scale origin.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_COLOR_TOOLTIP: Set the color used for rendering a shape.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_Z_TOOLTIP: Set the z order of a shape.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_X_TOOLTIP: Set the x coordinate of a shape.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_Y_TOOLTIP: Set the y coordinate of a shape.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_ROTATION_ANGLE_TOOLTI: Set the current rotation angle directly.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_FIX_TEXTURE_TOOLTIP: Choose whether texture coordinates stay fixed in world space.
RENDERER_TEXTURE_LOAD_TOOLTIP: Load a BMP texture from a path.
RENDERER_TEXTURE_SET_WRAP_MODE_TOOLTIP: Set how the texture wraps outside its bounds.
RENDERER_SHAPE_SET_TEXTURE_TOOLTIP: Attach a texture to a shape.
RENDERER_FONT_CONSTRUCTOR_TOOLTIP: Create a font object for drawing text.
RENDERER_SHAPES_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for renderer scenes, shapes, transforms, and collisions.
RENDERER_POLYGON_VERTEX_TOOLTIP: Create one [x, y] vertex for a polygon vertices list.
RENDERER_TEXTURE_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for renderer texture loading, wrapping, and texture values.
RENDERER_TEXTURE_GET_WIDTH_TOOLTIP: Get the loaded texture width in pixels.
RENDERER_TEXTURE_GET_HEIGHT_TOOLTIP: Get the loaded texture height in pixels.
RENDERER_TEXTURE_IS_VALID_TOOLTIP: Return non-zero when the texture contains valid image data.
RENDERER_FONT_DESCRIPTION: Blocks for renderer font values and glyph measurements.
RENDERER_FONT_GET_HEIGHT_TOOLTIP: Get the font height in pixels.
RENDERER_FONT_GET_CHAR_WIDTH_TOOLTIP: Get the width of one character glyph.
RENDERER_FONT_GET_CHAR_SPACING_TOOLTIP: Get spacing added after one character.
